Abstract

column2: An emulsion evaporation method was used to synthesize spherical poly(DL-lactide-co-glycolide)
(PLGA) nanoparticles with entrapped α-tocopherol. Two different surfactants were used:
sodium dodecyl sulfate (SDS) and poly(vinyl alcohol) (PVA). For SDS nanoparticles, the size
of the nanoparticles decreased signiﬁcantly with the entrapment of α-tocopherol in the PLGA
matrix, while the size of PVA nanoparticles remained unchanged. The polydispersity index
after synthesis was under 0.100 for PVA nanoparticles and around 0.150 for SDS nanoparticles.
The zeta potential was negative for all PVA nanoparticles. The entrapment efﬁciency of
α-tocopherol in the polymeric matrix was approximately 89% and 95% for nanoparticles with
8% and 16% α-tocopherol theoretical loading, respectively. The residual PVA associated with
the nanoparticles after puriﬁcation was approximately 6% (w/w relative to the nanoparticles).
The release proﬁle showed an initial burst followed by a slower release of the α-tocopherol
entrapped inside the PLGA matrix. The release for nanoparticles with 8% α-tocopherol
theoretical loading (86% released in the ﬁrst hour) was faster than the release for the
nanoparticles with 16% α-tocopherol theoretical loading (34% released in the ﬁrst hour).
